programmes.show_name: Can You Keep It Up For A Week?
programmes.show_description: Comedy. Annette will marry Gil, her hapless boyfriend, on condition he keeps his new job for a week without getting up to mischief with other women. Contains sexual scenes and 70s social attitudes.
1/4. Wildlife cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson returns home to Ireland on a personal quest to transform his old childhood garden into a haven for native wildlife.
With Empire Day approaching, a paddle steamer comes to the valley. The team pull out all the stops to put on a party for the tourists, including making clotted cream and scones.
2/20. An antiques expert, a market trader and an upcycler battle it out to make the most money. Charles Hanson, Linda Lambert and Zoe Pocock head to Glastonbury.

18/35. Charlie and Flo travel to Burton Latimer in Leicestershire to turn Rob and Gareth's new-build, empty plot into a Mediterranean, sociable space with room for their hot tub.
It's June, and the team heads up to Dartmoor to discover the ways in which Edwardian farmers took advantage of this unique and spectacular landscape to add to their income.

2/2. A 1940s fairground attraction, Saddam Hussein's fork and a pinball machine signed by Elton John come to the auction house.
Exploring the working lives of musicians working in London's West End from 1950s Soho, jazz clubs and theatre up to the present day.
DIY expert Dave Wellman is in Lancashire, fixing a stone cottage, patio wall. 15/20
DIY expert Dave Wellman is in Shropshire, demonstrating how to create more space in a bedroom .16/20
3/8. Revealing the shocking sugar content of some smoothies. What is meant by freshly-baked by supermarket breads. Comparing fresh and frozen food.

Comedy. A private eye's assistant tries to prove himself whilst the boss is away - solving a case involving blackmail, a spooky house, a mobster and a singer. Contains sex scenes.

Cinema projectionists make magic with light. As digital projection makes them obsolete, this film records the working lives of projectionists in the heyday of this dying art.
DIY expert Dave Wellman is in West Midlands, using reclaimed bricks during a masterclass in old-English brickwork. 17/20
DIY expert Dave Wellman is in Edinburgh. He fixes a window that needs shutters and fits a new sink.18/20
4/8. Stefan goes on a crash vegan diet for a month. Are veggie ready meals better for you? The surprising truth about imported vs home grown apples.
4/8. Under the hammer this time: a slice of royal wedding cake, a skeleton straight out of the wardrobe, a rare London tube map, and some vintage Soho sex-shop neon.

3/4. Colin cuts his wildflower meadow. With the flowering season coming to an end, it is time to remove his first year growth to make room for the native plants that lie waiting.
